sections.paragraph:
κατακύπτω 1 aor. κατέκυψα (s. κύπτω; Hom. et al.; Epict. 2, 16, 22; Lucian, Icarom. 15 al.; 4 Km 9:32; EpArist 91) bend down (Appian, Bell. Civ. 2, 62 §258 [ὑπʼ αἰδοῦς=out of shame]; Jos., Bell. 2, 224) J 8:8.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 519
